title: Document sharing in chat reuses the existing ACT share bundle plus a chat message and GSOC ping.
content:
## Pattern

Sharing a document through chat composes three existing primitives — no new protocol:

1. **Share bundle** — the existing `shareDocuments(docIds, recipientAddress)` creates the ACT-protected bundle on Swarm.
2. **Chat message** — a PSS message of type `share` carries a `DocumentShareAttachment` with the bundle's Swarm reference + a human-readable document list.
3. **GSOC notification** — a ping to the recipient's notify signer so their UI refreshes immediately rather than on next poll.

## Payload shape

```typescript
{
  type: "share",
  text: "Check out this invoice",
  timestamp: "2026-04-15T18:30:00Z",
  attachment: {
    kind: "document-share",
    driveId: "96ffdf26-...",
    driveName: "Q1 Invoices",
    shareReference: "abc123...",
    documents: [
      { id: "b9e4fe2e-...", name: "Invoice #42", type: "powerhouse/document-model" }
    ]
  }
}
```

## Adapter surface

The unified `buildDriveShareBundle()` in `plugin/sharing.ts` is shared between the Settings share panel and the chat share picker (`document-share-picker.tsx`). Both produce the same bundle shape and preserve sender signatures on every op — full parity with the Settings path.
description: A chat share sends three things: the existing shareDocuments() bundle, a PSS message of type share referencing it, and a GSOC notification so the recipient's UI reacts instantly.
